executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay gangs. This represents a coordinated, multi-group assault on Canadian infrastructure spanning healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and critical supply chains.

items.name: Qilin (Agenda)
items.targets: Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak - all Canadian organizations
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical ransomware crisis with 100+ organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors in the past 48 hours. Public sector targets include Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. Critical infrastructure in healthcare, education, telecommunications, and municipal services has been severely impacted. No CCCS advisories were identified in the provided feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer brand) compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi ransomware.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak by Everest ransomware group; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash compromised by Lynx; multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Reidhurst Nagy) also breached.

report_2w.executive_summary: Two critical vulnerabilities are actively being exploited in the wild: CVE-2026-12569 (PTC Windchill) and CVE-2026-20245 (Cisco SD-WAN Manager), both added to CISA's KEV catalog. Canada's national cybersecurity agency (CCCS) issued an alert about FortiBleed, a massive leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet credentials. Supply chain attacks are intensifying, with Miasma/Shai-Hulud malware targeting npm packages and GitHub Actions to steal cloud and CI/CD credentials. Ransomware attacks in Europe surged 55% in early 2026, with Akira and Qilin groups highly active, while healthcare and education sectors face significant breaches including Canvas and a Japanese telecom exposing 14.2 million passwords.

items.description: Critical vulnerability in PTC Windchill allowing remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Actively exploited Cisco vulnerability added to CISA KEV catalog
Authenticated local attacker can execute arbitrary commands on Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager; exploited months before disclosure
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: NEC ExpressUpdate Agent for Windows exposes IOCTL enabling local privilege escalation to SYSTEM
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: Miasma / Shai-Hulud
items.targets: npm package ecosystem, GitHub Actions, Go ecosystem, LeoPlatform and RStreams packages, cloud and CI/CD infrastructure
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1078 (Valid Accounts - credential theft), malicious package injection

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Precise Forms, multiple organizations across Europe as part of 55% surge in ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion tactics

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: European organizations as part of ransomware surge targeting supply chains
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1199 (Trusted Relationship - supply chain focus)

items.name: KongTuke
items.targets: Organizations via ClickFix and ModeloRAT campaigns
items.ttps: T1204.001 (User Execution: Malicious Link via ClickFix social engineering), Mistic backdoor deployment

items.name: LemonDuck
items.targets: Organizations via PowerShell-based cryptomining campaigns
items.ttps: T1059.001 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell), T1496 (Resource Hijacking - cryptomining)

items.name: Photo ZIP / Node.js Implant Campaign
items.targets: Hospitality industry including hotels
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1059.007 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript/Node.js), persistent backdoor

items.name: Mirage2FA
items.targets: Microsoft 365 users
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1027.006 (Obfuscated Files or Information: HTML Smuggling), AiTM (Adversary-in-the-Middle)

items.name: Shopify Shop App Phishing Campaign
items.targets: Shopify users via callback phishing injected into legitimate purchase receipts
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1534 (Internal Spearphishing - injection into legitimate app notifications)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: ADT, Inc. (US government and defense sector organization)
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), data leak operations

items.name: Turla (Russian APT)
items.targets: Intelligence gathering operations with STOCKSTAY backdoor
items.ttps: T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1082 (System Information Discovery), sophisticated APT tradecraft

items.name: CL-STA-1062 Campaign
items.targets: Southeast Asian government and critical infrastructure
items.ttps: T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), TinyRCT backdoor, hybrid toolkit targeting critical infrastructure

items.name: Iranian-Turkish National (arrested)
items.targets: Multiple victims in $3.4 billion hacking scheme
items.ttps: Hacking, conspiracy, wire fraud charges filed in Southern District of New York

items.name: SIM-Swapping Gang (Poland-based)
items.targets: Cryptocurrency holders for theft operations
items.ttps: T1556.003 (Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication), SIM swapping attacks

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued Alert AL26-014 regarding FortiBleed, a massive leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials impacting Canadian organizations using Fortinet infrastructure. One ransomware group (INTERLOCK) claimed an attack on Clearview Eye Centre, a Calgary-based ophthalmology clinic. Royal Bank of Canada was fined $4.25 million over incorrect credit card statements.
canada_landscape.financial: Royal Bank of Canada fined $4.25 million over incorrect credit card statements (regulatory issue, not cyber incident)
